name: n8n
license: Sustainable Use License / fair-code style; review before bundling or redistribution
what: Workflow automation platform for API orchestration, webhooks, ETL-style workflows, integrations, and agentic automations.
solves: Low-code/high-flexibility automation across webhooks, SaaS APIs, internal apps, queues, and scheduled tasks without hardcoding every integration into the site.
users: Ops, growth, engineering, and integration teams that need fast automation and event choreography.
fit: Very high fit. Hylono already cares about n8n integration, and it is the natural glue between Next.js APIs, Twenty, Novu, Stripe, Cal.com, documents, and internal admin tooling.
runtime: Node.js automation platform with workflow engine, database, workers/queue mode, webhook ingestion, and credentials store.
components: Editor/UI, execution engine, DB, optional queue workers, Redis for queue mode, webhooks, credentials store, and integrations/nodes.
deploy: Docker is standard; production may use queue mode with Redis, separate workers, persistent database, and public webhook reachability.
deps: Database, optional Redis for queue mode, webhook ingress, mail/notifications, secrets handling, and storage for binary data if used.
datastores: Database for workflows/executions plus optional Redis for queue mode.
auth: Built-in user auth, API keys, credential vaulting inside n8n, and webhook auth patterns.
apis: REST API, webhook triggers, MCP trigger, hundreds of nodes/connectors, and import/export of workflows.
security: Extremely powerful blast radius. Protect credentials, isolate environments, restrict who can edit workflows, and avoid sensitive payload sprawl in executions.
setup_path: Use official self-host docs; deploy with persistent DB, reverse proxy/TLS, and public webhook routing. Add queue mode only when concurrency or reliability requires it.
env: DB settings, encryption key, webhook URL, host/base URL, Redis settings if queue mode, execution retention, and credential secrets.
persistence: Back up database and export important workflows; review binary data retention and execution logs.
operations: Webhook delivery, failed execution handling, credential rotation, workflow versioning, and worker scaling.
failure_points: Using it as an ungoverned shadow backend, leaking secrets into workflow nodes, and failing to test webhook callbacks end to end.
integration: First-class fit with Next.js routes, Stripe, Resend, Novu, Twenty, Cal.com, Documenso, Lago, PostHog, Slack/internal alerts, and future AI/ops flows.
verify: Define clear system boundaries: which automations stay in n8n versus in application code. Decide who owns workflow review and credential governance.
risk: Moderate. Huge leverage, but it can create invisible technical debt if not governed.
gdpr: Workflow payloads can capture personal data. Apply retention controls, redact where possible, and choose EU-hosted deployment.
overlap: Overlaps with Node-RED for automation; n8n is the better default for business/system workflows in this stack.
codex_read_first: Read current Next.js API routes, webhook/event map, and n8n production docs before building flows.
codex_best_path: Use n8n as the integration/orchestration layer around the core app, not as a substitute for domain logic that belongs in the app codebase.
codex_never_assume: Never assume local-test webhooks or tunnel behavior represent production. Never assume execution data can be retained indefinitely.

assets.name: Self-hosted AI starter kit
assets.notes: Useful reference for containerized deployments and AI add-ons.
